provenance.tool: PixelLab (Tier 1) + Python(Pillow) compositing; SVG diagrams hand-authored
provenance.license_note: PixelLab-generated assets subject to PixelLab Terms of Service (https://pixellab.ai/termsofservice). Verify commercial/coursework rights before external reuse.

assets.label: Elijah
assets.role: narrator; ex-tech worker learning to build

assets.label: The Field Kit
assets.alt: A pixel-art specimen board of the field kit: photobioreactor, grow tower, LoRa node, solar and battery, 3D printer, CNC plasma table, walk-behind tractor, cyberdeck
assets.caption: The field kit. Every one of these already exists, and a person with modest money and stubbornness can own the lot.

assets.label: The Co-op
assets.alt: Pixel-art portraits of the co-op: Elijah, Marta, Priya, and Denny
assets.caption: The people the rest of this book is about. Nineteen households; these four you get to know.

assets.label: The Photobioreactor (solo)
assets.alt: A DIY 3D-printed photobioreactor growing green algae with a Raspberry Pi board
assets.caption: Tens of thousands of dollars in a lab. A few hundred on a workbench.

assets.label: The Cyberdeck (solo)
assets.alt: A homemade cyberdeck: rugged case, mechanical keyboard, amber map screen, antenna
assets.caption: A computer you understand all the way down, that answers to nobody's server.

assets.label: You Do Not Program an Ox
assets.alt: A brown ox in a wooden yoke pulling a plow through a field
assets.caption: The first time labor could be handed to something that ran on its own. The machine is the second.

assets.label: Two Supply Lines: Global vs Hyper-local
assets.alt: Two supply lines compared: a long fragile global chain versus a short hyper-local loop
assets.caption: Not decentralization for its own sake. A shorter line you can hold in your head.

assets.label: The Closed Algae Fish-Food Loop
assets.alt: The closed algae fish-food loop: fish, grow beds, dry and grind, photobioreactor, back to fish
assets.caption: Grow the fish food too, and the last store-bought input drops out. The loop closes.

assets.label: Boston Dynamics Atlas during DARPA testing
assets.alt: Boston Dynamics Atlas during DARPA testing
assets.caption: Atlas during testing. The data does not care how you feel about it.

assets.label: Honda ASIMO humanoid robot, 2001
assets.alt: Honda ASIMO humanoid robot, 2001
assets.caption: ASIMO, 2001. The weirdness arrived on schedule.

assets.label: Peeling red bark of a Pacific madrone (Arbutus menziesii)
assets.alt: Peeling red bark of a Pacific madrone (Arbutus menziesii)
assets.caption: Pacific madrone, mid-shed. It burns to the ground and comes back from the root.

assets.label: Part I Divider: Event Horizon

placement.section: Part I: What is the Singularity?

assets.alt: Pixel art: a dark sun crossed by an amber ring rising over a perspective grid, a lone figure standing at the horizon

assets.label: Event Horizon Telescope image of the M87 supermassive black hole
assets.alt: Event Horizon Telescope image of the M87 supermassive black hole
assets.caption: The event horizon of M87. Past a certain line, there is no going back.

assets.label: Robotic arms assembling a car body
assets.alt: Robotic arms assembling a car body
assets.caption: Robotic arms at work. The structural automation of labor, already routine.

assets.label: Falcon Heavy lifting off from Kennedy Space Center

placement.section: Chapter 3: The Leap to ASI & the Machine Exodus (Stages 6–7)

assets.alt: Falcon Heavy lifting off from Kennedy Space Center
assets.caption: Falcon Heavy demo mission. The exodus hardware is already flying.

assets.label: Hubble Ultra-Deep Field showing thousands of galaxies
assets.alt: Hubble Ultra-Deep Field showing thousands of galaxies
assets.caption: The Hubble Ultra-Deep Field. The available real estate.

assets.label: The Sun imaged by NASA's Solar Dynamics Observatory
assets.alt: The Sun imaged by NASA's Solar Dynamics Observatory
assets.caption: The Sun, via NASA's Solar Dynamics Observatory. The only income the planet has.

assets.label: The Closed-Loop Survival Cycle

placement.section: 2. Digital Leverage: Content as Energy Arbitrage

assets.alt: The Closed-Loop Survival Cycle: sun and soil feed community calories, which fund creative autonomy, which buys physical hardware, which expands the community's capture of sun and soil
assets.caption: The Closed-Loop Survival Cycle. Energy captured from the sun and soil stays inside the community, and every pass through the loop widens the intake.

assets.label: Part II Divider: The Fire Circle

placement.section: Part II: How Humans React: The Cooperative Transition

assets.alt: Pixel art: scattered figures standing alone in the dark on the left, a small group gathered around a warm fire on the right

assets.label: Edmond de Belamy, the first AI-generated portrait sold at a major auction house

placement.section: Chapter 6: The Singularity Is a Done Deal

assets.alt: Edmond de Belamy, the first AI-generated portrait sold at a major auction house
assets.caption: Edmond de Belamy, generated by a GAN, sold at Christie's for $432,500 in 2018.

assets.label: Chess board set up in the opening position

placement.section: Chapter 7: The Battle Lines: The Entitled vs. The Ready

assets.alt: Chess board set up in the opening position
assets.caption: Opening position. The battle lines are drawn before most players notice the game started.

assets.label: Sophia, the humanoid robot by Hanson Robotics

placement.section: Chapter 8: The Psychology of the Collapse

assets.alt: Sophia, the humanoid robot by Hanson Robotics
assets.caption: Sophia. The mirror humans keep flinching at.

assets.label: The Cognitive Extraction Engine
assets.alt: The Cognitive Extraction Engine: raw attention is harvested by infinite scroll and notifications, depleting the prefrontal cortex, producing systemic suggestibility, and ending in loss of physical agency
assets.caption: The Cognitive Extraction Engine. Attention goes in at the top; agency comes out the bottom, and the platform keeps the difference.

assets.label: Information Dispensing Architectures
assets.alt: Information Dispensing Architectures: a centralized algorithmic stream interrupting your brain constantly, versus filtered local sources delivered to your brain in scheduled batches
assets.caption: Two architectures for the same brain. The stream keeps you reactive; the batch keeps you sovereign.

assets.label: The Cognitive Fragmentation Curve
assets.alt: The Cognitive Fragmentation Curve: a graph of cognitive depth over one hour, showing a twenty-minute climb to deep-focus baseline, then repeated algorithmic interrupts, each recovery peaking lower than the last
assets.caption: The Cognitive Fragmentation Curve. Above roughly three interrupts an hour, the curve never touches baseline again.

assets.label: The Cognitive Firewall Pipeline

placement.section: Semantic Hygiene: Protecting the Input Buffer

assets.alt: The Cognitive Firewall Pipeline: the high-entropy digital web passes through strict batch processing, then text-only synthesis, and emerges as deep physical execution
assets.caption: The Cognitive Firewall Pipeline. Entropy falls left to right; agency rises.

assets.label: The Decay of Isolated Systems

placement.section: Section 1: The Thermodynamic Failure of Isolation

assets.alt: The Decay of Isolated Systems: an isolated individual fans out into solar drift, soil decline, and cognitive exhaustion, which converge into system collapse at maximum entropy
assets.caption: The Decay of Isolated Systems. One person cannot out-work entropy on every front at once.

assets.label: Trust network topologies
assets.alt: Trust network topologies: a star topology where every node depends on one hub that can fail, versus a mesh topology where every node connects to several neighbors and damage becomes a detour
assets.caption: Star versus mesh. In the star, the hub's failure is everyone's failure; in the mesh, damage is a routing problem.

assets.label: The Autonomous Commons Loop
assets.alt: The Autonomous Commons Loop: a shared solar DC microgrid powers a local GPU server producing cognitive output and a FarmBot bioreactor producing caloric yield, both feeding the human trust mesh, which maintains the grid in return
assets.caption: The Autonomous Commons Loop. Energy becomes intelligence and calories, and both flow back into the mesh that keeps the panels clean.

assets.label: Desktop 3D printer mid-print

placement.section: Chapter 9: Deglobalization and the Neighborhood Factory

assets.alt: Desktop 3D printer mid-print
assets.caption: A desktop 3D printer. The neighborhood factory fits on a workbench.

assets.label: The Destructive NPK Feedback Loop

placement.section: Section 1: Demolishing the Petrochemical NPK Trap

assets.alt: The Destructive NPK Feedback Loop: applying synthetic NPK kills native soil biology, which collapses the soil into sterile dirt, which makes plants dependent on constant synthetic inputs, which sends you back to buy the next bag
assets.caption: The NPK subscription trap. Every bag makes the next bag mandatory; the exit is biological.

assets.label: The Greenhouse Automation Bus

placement.section: Section 2: The Automated Greenhouse Core (Mycodo and Raspberry Pi)

assets.alt: The Greenhouse Automation Bus: a Raspberry Pi running Mycodo reads an I2C sensor bus (SHT31-D air, STEMMA soil) and drives a GPIO relay board controlling a water solenoid, exhaust fan, and grow lights, in a closed sense-decide-actuate loop
assets.caption: The Greenhouse Automation Bus. Sensors in on I2C, 12V actuators out on GPIO relays, PID loops in the middle.

assets.label: The CNC Autonomous Bed

placement.section: Section 3: The Mechanical Calorie Engine (FarmBot Architecture)

assets.alt: The CNC Autonomous Bed: a gantry travels the X-axis over a dense raised bed while a Z-axis tool head seeds, waters, and weeds plants addressed by X-Y coordinate, with no walking paths wasted
assets.caption: The CNC Autonomous Bed. Every plant is a coordinate; the machine tends the whole grid from one rail.

assets.label: The ACT Biological Reactor

placement.section: Section 4: The Aerated Compost Tea (ACT) Bioreactor

assets.alt: The ACT Biological Reactor: a mesh compost bag suspended in a 200-liter tank of water, molasses, and kelp solution, aerated from below by a 12V linear diaphragm air pump, with a dissolved-oxygen gauge that must stay above 6 mg/L
assets.caption: The ACT Bioreactor. Keep the dissolved oxygen above 6 mg/L or the brew turns to a pathogen soup; the pump is not optional.

assets.label: Part III Divider: The Shouse Homestead

placement.section: Part III: How to Survive the Transition

assets.alt: Pixel art: a container shouse with a lit window and rooftop solar, a wind turbine, garden rows, and a LoRa mast under a starry sky

assets.label: The New York Stock Exchange trading floor
assets.alt: The New York Stock Exchange trading floor
assets.caption: The trading floor. The consumption engine you are stepping out of.

assets.label: Model collapse generation by generation
assets.alt: Model collapse generation by generation: a wide, rich human-data distribution at Generation 0 narrows to a piled-up mean at Generation 1 and finally to a single degenerate spike at Generation 5
assets.caption: Model collapse across one training lineage. The rich tails, the edge cases, the genius, are pruned first and gone by Generation 5.

assets.label: The Algorithmic Judo pipeline

placement.section: Algorithmic Judo: Weaponizing the Feedback Loop

assets.alt: The Algorithmic Judo pipeline: a high-utility off-grid tech core wrapped in an outrage or tragedy narrative hook is scored viral by the algorithm and delivered to a human target who unlocks the core
assets.caption: Algorithmic Judo. A dry manual scores near zero; wrapped in a human story, the algorithm carries it for you.

assets.label: Boston Dynamics Spot quadruped robot

placement.section: Chapter 11: Using the Tech to Your Advantage

assets.alt: Boston Dynamics Spot quadruped robot
assets.caption: Spot. The same machines ending careers will happily work for you.

assets.label: The Autonomous Rack Architecture

placement.section: Section 1: Sourcing and Hardware Mathematics (Independent Compute)

assets.alt: The Autonomous Rack Architecture: a 12V-native rack with an EdgeRouter and DC switch at U1, a 24V-to-12V fuse panel at U2, a headless Proxmox node with two 24 GB GPUs in the middle, and a coolant pump and reservoir at the bottom
assets.caption: The Autonomous Rack. Everything runs native 12V DC off the shell's bus: no inverter, no wall bricks, no meter.

assets.label: The Split-Loop Thermal Exchange

placement.section: Section 2: Custom Thermodynamic Cooling (External Heat Exchange)

assets.alt: The Split-Loop Thermal Exchange: GPU and CPU water blocks inside the insulated shell feed a 12V pump that sends hot coolant through the wall to a salvaged car radiator in free air, with cool coolant returning to the chips
assets.caption: The Split-Loop Thermal Exchange. Pull the heat straight off the silicon and dump it outside, instead of paying an AC unit to fight your own server.

assets.label: FarmBot automated gardening system over a raised bed
assets.alt: FarmBot automated gardening system over a raised bed
assets.caption: FarmBot over a raised bed. Land plus automation is the position to hold.

assets.label: The Shouse Thermodynamic Engine

placement.section: Section 1: The Anatomy of the Shouse (The Productive Node)

assets.alt: The Shouse Thermodynamic Engine: the shop half holds the welder, CNC plasma, tool library, server Crucible, DC fuse block, and rainwater rack; a shared thermal and power bus feeds the insulated house half below
assets.caption: The Shouse. Half factory, half home, one envelope: the shop's waste heat is the house's furnace.

assets.label: The Semi-Autonomous CSA Network
assets.alt: The Semi-Autonomous CSA Network: Shouse Node A with its AI server and automated greenhouse, Shouse Node B with its tool library and battery bank, exchanging tasks over LoRa mesh, and a vulnerable node receiving automated calorie and labor deliveries
assets.caption: The Semi-Autonomous CSA. Automated nodes run at roughly 300% of their own need; the surplus carries whoever cannot produce.

assets.label: Solar panels on a rural outbuilding roof
assets.alt: Solar panels on a rural outbuilding roof
assets.caption: Solar on an outbuilding. The Shouse Grid starts with one roof.

assets.label: The Double-Wide Shell Architecture

placement.section: The Autonomous Shell: The $25,000 Exit Strategy

assets.alt: The Double-Wide Shell Architecture: two 8-by-40-foot containers mated along a reinforced seam, one side holding the workspace and server room, the other the wet-room, kitchen, and living quarters
assets.caption: The Double-Wide Shell. Two one-trip containers, one surgical cut, 640 square feet of open span.

assets.label: 6-Point Pier Alignment

placement.section: Step 1: Site Prep, Excavation, and the Pier Layout

assets.alt: 6-Point Pier Alignment: plan view of six concrete piers positioned at the corner and center points of both containers' outer rails, with the seam line running between them
assets.caption: The 6-point pier layout. Corner and center points, dug past the frost line, leveled to the millimeter.

assets.label: Center C-Channel Reinforcement

placement.section: Step 3: The Surgical Cut and Structural Reinforcement

assets.alt: Center C-Channel Reinforcement: section view showing the roof corrugation carried by a double C-channel header beam on 6 mm weld plates, supported by 75 by 75 mm HSS vertical columns welded to the floor rails
assets.caption: The center-seam skeleton. Columns first, header second, then the cut: the roof never carries itself.

assets.label: The Thermal Barrier Layer

placement.section: Step 4: The Thermodynamics of Insulation (Beating the Tin Can)

assets.alt: The Thermal Barrier Layer: wall section showing heat from the exterior hitting the 2 mm steel wall, being absorbed by 75 mm of closed-cell spray foam rated R-21, and blocked before the interior wood panel
assets.caption: The thermal barrier in section. Foam bonded to bare steel: vapor-sealed, condensation-proof, and the heat stays outside.

assets.label: Universal Wet-Room Layout

placement.section: Step 5: Universal Mechanical Design (The Wet-Room Protocol)

assets.alt: Universal Wet-Room Layout: plan view with DC water pump feeding a shower fixture, the entire floor sloping as one basin toward a linear drain, and greywater exiting to the garden beds
assets.caption: The Universal Wet-Room. One sloped basin, one linear drain, zero thresholds to trip over at any age.

assets.label: Interior of an abandoned shopping mall

placement.section: Chapter 14: The Collapse of the Long Tail

assets.alt: Interior of an abandoned shopping mall
assets.caption: A dead mall. Gradually, then suddenly.

assets.label: The Logistic Shockwave
assets.alt: The Logistic Shockwave: a four-day cascade from port disruption, to trucks rerouted, to shelves stripped clean, to systemic panic, with the local food buffer draining to zero alongside
assets.caption: The Logistic Shockwave. A just-in-time system has no buffer to absorb the blow, so the blow travels straight through to the shelves.

assets.label: Centralized versus decentralized mesh communications
assets.alt: Centralized versus decentralized mesh communications: a single tower serving three users fails entirely when broken, while a mesh of nodes routes around any dead node
assets.caption: Two topologies for a town. The tower is a single point of failure; the mesh treats damage as a routing problem.

assets.label: Automated watering over garden soil

placement.section: Chapter 15: The Power of Reclaiming Soil

assets.alt: Automated watering over garden soil
assets.caption: Automated watering over living soil. The substrate everything else depends on.

assets.label: The Cooperative Land Trust Firewall

placement.section: The Legal Firewall: Hacking the Deed

assets.alt: The Cooperative Land Trust Firewall: the speculative real estate market's attempts to buy or foreclose are blocked by a legal shield; inside the trust, the land is held in perpetuity and leased to community stewards on a 99-year ground lease
assets.caption: The CLT firewall. Split the title, and the market can no longer reach the dirt.

assets.label: The Soil Food Web Engine

placement.section: The Biological Hack: Bypassing the Petrochemical Cartel

assets.alt: The Soil Food Web Engine: sunlight and plants pump sugars to bacteria and fungi, which mine minerals, which are digested by protozoa and nematodes, which release plant-available nutrients back to the plants in a closed loop
assets.caption: The Soil Food Web. A biological refinery running on sunlight: the plants feed the web that feeds the plants.

assets.label: Racks of servers in a data center

placement.section: Chapter 16: Digital Leverage and Media Autonomy

assets.alt: Racks of servers in a data center
assets.caption: Server racks. Code and media scale while you sleep.

assets.label: The Spiral of Model Collapse

placement.section: The Great Slop-ocalypse: Why the Machine Needs You

assets.alt: The Spiral of Model Collapse: AI models train on high-quality human data, flood the web with synthetic slop, then train on their own slop, degrading with every lap unless fresh human data enters the loop
assets.caption: The Spiral of Model Collapse. Every lap around the loop: less variance, more mush.

assets.label: The Algorithmic Judo Pipeline
assets.alt: The Algorithmic Judo Pipeline: a trojan-horse media package with a visible emotional wrapper and a hidden tactical payload rides the engagement algorithm to wide reach, then delivers autonomy guides, coordination tools, and off-grid manuals
assets.caption: Algorithmic Judo. The algorithm boosts the wrapper; the wrapper carries the payload.

assets.label: The Sneakernet Cycle
assets.alt: The Sneakernet Cycle: a hyper-local node encrypts data onto a microSD card, which is passed by hand or local courier to a neighbor node that decrypts it, and the cycle repeats in reverse
assets.caption: The Sneakernet Cycle. A terabyte walks across town in a pocket, and nobody upstream ever sees a packet.

assets.label: Workbench with hand tools
assets.alt: Workbench with hand tools
assets.caption: The workbench. Own the tools, own the output.

assets.label: Anatomy of a hyper-local mesh node

placement.section: 1. The Nervous System: DIY LoRa Mesh Nodes

assets.alt: Anatomy of a hyper-local mesh node: a waterproof Pelican case containing a 5-watt solar panel feeding two salvaged 18650 batteries feeding an ESP32 LoRa chip, with a tuned antenna broadcasting to the next node on 915 or 868 MHz
assets.caption: A hyper-local mesh node. Sunlight in, encrypted packets out, no license and no bill.

assets.label: The inverter trap versus the DC-native protocol

placement.section: 2. The Muscle: DC-Native Solar and Battery Microgrids

assets.alt: The inverter trap versus the DC-native protocol: the conventional chain loses 10 to 15 percent converting DC to AC and again converting back to DC at the wall brick, while the DC-native chain runs solar to battery to fuse block to devices with zero conversion loss
assets.caption: The round-trip toll. Every load in the shell already wanted DC; the inverter chain charges you twice to deliver it.

assets.label: The Neighborhood Fab Lab
assets.alt: The Neighborhood Fab Lab: recycled raw materials feed three open-source machine cores, a RepRap 3D printer, a CNC plasma router, and an open-source tractor, producing custom parts, structural steel, and field power
assets.caption: The Neighborhood Fab Lab. Broken means fabricate, not wait.

assets.label: Topographic map with compass rose

placement.section: Chapter 18: The Roadmap and the Premortem Pivot

assets.alt: Topographic map with compass rose
assets.caption: The map, before the route. Premortem first, then move.

assets.label: Sunrise seen from the International Space Station
assets.alt: Sunrise seen from the International Space Station
assets.caption: Sunrise from orbit. The new era does not ask permission.

assets.label: The Surplus Redirection Matrix
assets.alt: The Surplus Redirection Matrix: commercial surplus food is sorted by edibility, with edible food redirected to municipal kitchens and CLT food banks for utility credits, non-edible organics routed to the soil food web bioreactor, and attempted landfill dumping met with a progressive civil penalty funding the MRSF
assets.caption: The Surplus Redirection Matrix. Every path out of a loading dock leads somewhere useful, including the penalty.

assets.label: The nine stages of the singularity, grouped into three eras
assets.alt: The nine stages of the singularity, grouped into three eras: the Era of AGI (stages 1 through 5), the Leap to ASI (stages 6 and 7), and the Universal and Multiverse Apex (stages 8 and 9), with Stage 4, the New Social Contract, highlighted as the hinge
assets.caption: The whole roadmap on one page. Everything you can act on lives in the first five stages, and the fight is over the terms of Stage 4.

assets.label: A dense city core ringed by four surrounding municipalities that supply it

placement.section: A Dense City Cannot Feed Itself, and It Does Not Have To

assets.alt: A dense city core ringed by four surrounding municipalities that supply it: food production, materials recovery, energy and water, and neighborhood factories, with supply flowing inward to the core
assets.caption: You do not deglobalize a city by walling it off. You ring it with municipalities that produce what it cannot, which is also where the good jobs are.

assets.label: Two social contracts compared side by side
assets.alt: Two social contracts compared side by side: the old one runs your labor to a wage to rented survival, and cuts you off if you miss a payment; the new one runs machines and regenerative loops to local abundance to an adequate level of care, so a job becomes a choice
assets.caption: The old contract makes you rent your survival month to month. The new one makes survival the floor, so a job becomes a choice, not a sentence.

assets.label: The Robots That Grow Your Food and Build Your House
assets.alt: A pixel-art diptych: on the left a solar-topped farm robot on treads waters a row of vegetable seedlings; on the right a robot on treads raises and bolts the timber frame of a small house
assets.caption: The two machines this whole book is about: one that grows the food, one that builds the house. The pieces already exist; the work is putting them in your neighbors' hands.
assets.provenance: PixelLab 1-direction objects + Python(Pillow) compositing on the navy/amber palette

assets.label: Gaming Rig (GPU Tower)
assets.alt: A beige mini-tower PC case with two mismatched GPUs zip-tied inside, visible through an open side panel
assets.caption: The rig. Two GPUs zip-tied into a case that never fit them. It throttles by design.

assets.label: The Fisher Wood Stove
assets.alt: A squat black cast-iron wood stove with the door open, one cracked firebrick visible inside
assets.caption: The Fisher. Eighty pounds of steel, one cracked firebrick. Older than him.

assets.label: Shadow-Board Tool Wall
assets.alt: A pegboard tool wall with hand tools hung in painted shadow-board silhouettes
assets.caption: The tool wall. Shadow-boarded like an evidence display.

assets.label: Field-Repairable Controller
assets.alt: A yellow PETG 3D-printed greenhouse controller box, lid open, a Raspberry Pi and screw terminals visible inside
assets.caption: The controller. Field-repairable. Screw terminals instead of poetry.

assets.label: The Crucible (Rack + Radiator)

placement.section: The Crucible: Local Servers and Air-Gapped Sandboxes

assets.alt: A stack of three scavenged rack-mount servers beside a salvaged car radiator with brass pipe fittings
assets.caption: The Crucible. Three filing cabinets and a salvaged radiator.

assets.label: The Bench Vise
assets.alt: A heavy steel bench vise clamping a broken metal weld test coupon, the fracture bright and visible
assets.caption: The vise. The metal does the truth. Bright and crystalline.

assets.label: The Hardware-Store Bell

placement.section: the absorption of Frank's hardware store

assets.alt: A brass bell mounted above a worn wooden hardware-store door frame
assets.caption: The bell. Frank's grandfather hung it over the door.

assets.label: Three-Depth Soil Probe
assets.alt: A white PVC pipe soil-moisture probe with three depth-marked sensor leads
assets.caption: The probe. Three depths, five, fifteen, thirty centimeters.

assets.label: The Printed Proofs
assets.alt: An open cardboard box of freshly printed perfect-bound paperback books
assets.caption: The proofs. Ten copies, perfect-bound. The book, finally.

assets.label: Reuben Vance
assets.alt: A lean man in a well-tailored dark coat, carrying a leather folio of papers
assets.caption: Reuben Vance. Handles the part of the machine that runs on paper.

assets.label: Frank Kowalski
assets.role: hardware-store owner, absorbed into the co-op

assets.label: Frank Kowalski
assets.alt: An older hardware-store owner wearing glasses and a canvas work apron
assets.caption: Frank Kowalski. Knows the torque spec from memory.

assets.label: Gary

placement.section: Marta tells Gary and Sal's story

assets.alt: A middle-aged factory worker with a tired anxious expression, wearing a plant uniform
assets.caption: Gary. Stayed informed. Couldn't fix a fence.

assets.label: Devendra
assets.alt: A tech worker in business casual clothing with a laptop bag over one shoulder
assets.caption: Devendra. Senior and calm. Wrong about the thing that mattered.
